query: Our existing essay rubric has 8 criteria with band descriptors like 'good,' 'excellent,' 'satisfactory,' and 'needs improvement.' Two TAs grade the same essay independently and consistently disagree by 1-2 bands per criterion across 10 essays. The students are starting to appeal. Help me fix the rubric so the disagreement drops.
expected_behavior: Diagnoses the root causes — 8 criteria is above the 4-6 sweet spot (graders cannot hold all of them in working memory) AND the band descriptors ('good,' 'excellent,' 'satisfactory') do not name observable evidence so two graders interpret them differently.
Recommends collapsing the 8 criteria to 4-5 that map to the assignment's learning objectives without overlap, NOT just rewording the existing 8.

Rewrites at least one band descriptor with observable evidence appropriate to the essay artifact (e.g., for an argument criterion: 'thesis stated and defended with ≥ 2 supporting points each backed by a cited source' rather than 'excellent argument').
